task_name: Builder runtime entry: health and DB readiness probe
goal: Add a minimal HTTP runtime entry to bluepilot/builder using only Node's built-in http module: a liveness route that returns 200 independent of the database, and a DB-readiness route that pings the real Neon database via getDb() and reports reachable or not without crashing, so Render has a real, safe process to start.
scope_out: Do not add any dependency; use only Node's built-in http module, so builder/package.json changes are limited to adding a start script and builder/package-lock.json stays untouched.

Do not expose any builder-execution route; server.ts must not import opusTaskOrchestrator, opusBuildPipeline, builderExecutor, builderGithubBridge or the maya gate. No build can be triggered over HTTP in BP-132.
Do not make the liveness route depend on the database; only the readiness route touches the DB.
Do not require real API keys or a reachable maya-core.
Do not create the Render service in this task; that is a later operator infra action. BP-132 only builds the process Render will start.
Do not modify soulmatch, root governance, root package.json, or builder/tsconfig.json.
claims: builder/src/server.ts starts an HTTP server on process.env.PORT (with a local fallback port) using only the built-in node:http module.
GET /health returns HTTP 200 with a small JSON body and never calls getDb(), so it succeeds even when the database is missing or unreachable.

GET /health/db calls getDb() and runs a trivial SELECT-1 style query, wrapped in try/catch, and reports a JSON readiness status: reachable, not_configured (missing BLUEPILOT_BUILDER_DATABASE_URL), or unreachable, without crashing the process.
The readiness handler logic lives in builder/src/health.ts so it can be unit-tested without binding a real port.
No new dependency is added; builder/package.json gains only a start script (tsx src/server.ts) and builder/package-lock.json is unchanged.
server.ts imports no builder-execution or maya-gate module; there is no HTTP path that can trigger a build.
assumptions: Render will be configured later with root directory builder/, build command npm install, start command npm start, plus PORT and BLUEPILOT_BUILDER_DATABASE_URL env vars.
The exact drizzle/neon-http readiness query form is an implementation detail Codex picks so it typechecks; the intent is a trivial SELECT 1.
The local runtime check against the real Neon database can be run now without Render, since the database already exists from BP-131.
dod: builder/src/server.ts exists and uses only node:http; it reads PORT from the environment with a local fallback.
GET /health/db returns a JSON readiness status and never throws out of the request handler; missing URL yields not_configured, a failing query yields unreachable.
builder/src/health.ts holds the readiness logic and is imported by both server.ts and the test.

builder/tests/health.test.ts runs without real API keys, without a live database, and without a reachable maya-core: it asserts /health is 200 and that the readiness handler returns not_configured when BLUEPILOT_BUILDER_DATABASE_URL is unset.
server.ts imports no builder-execution module or maya gate (verified by inspection).
builder/package.json adds only a start script; builder/package-lock.json shows no diff.
npm test in builder/ passes; npm run typecheck passes.
Runtime check (operator, local, with the real Neon URL set): npm start boots, GET /health returns 200, GET /health/db reports reachable. Result recorded in review-packets/BP-132.md.
builder/README.md documents the Render service settings (root directory, build/start commands, required PORT and BLUEPILOT_BUILDER_DATABASE_URL).
node tools/verify-task-lock.cjs BP-132 --verify exits 0; git diff --check exits 0.
stop_conditions: A dependency would be needed (anything beyond node:http).
The liveness route would need the database to answer.
Any builder-execution or maya-gate module would have to be imported to satisfy the task.
builder/package-lock.json, root package.json, builder/tsconfig.json, or root governance would have to change.
soulmatch would need to be changed.
prior_task_findings: BP-130 completed the verbatim Builder core migration; BP-131 documented the live Neon DB (15 tables verified) but deliberately did not create the Render service. BP-132 is the first non-verbatim task, so the strict order returns to build -> push -> STOP -> VAL-K2 -> merge. Security note from BP-131 VAL-K2: the repo is public and the BP-131 review packet exposed Neon resource identifiers (host endpoint, role, project IDs); the connection-string password was not committed. A scrub remains an optional separate task; do not bundle it into BP-132.
